text: Neural machine translation (NMT) is a new statistical approach to machine translation that uses a neural network. Most NMT models use a encoder-decoder framework. The encoder takes the input sentence and outputs a vector of fixed length, while the decoder takes the vector and outputs the translation of the input sentence. In this paper, we studied two neural machine translation models, namely RNN Encoder-Decoder and a new model called Gated Recursive Convolutional Neural Network (GRCNN) for machine translation. Our experiments show that the NMT models perform reasonably well on short sentences with no unknown words, but the performance drops quickly as the sentence length and number of unknown words increase. Overall, the proposed GRCNN model can learn the grammar structure of the input sentence automatically.
explanation: Text is cohesive and uses standard NMT terminology, which is common in AI-generated summaries. The mention of a possibly obscure 'Gated Recursive Convolutional Neural Network (GRCNN)' model could indicate synthetic generation, leading to a moderate probability.
